observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the LCE agreed to:
Direct the MA to disclose information on the LRN in the Notes to FS;
Provide resources/manpower to the Municipal Engineer in assisting the MAO and MGSO in identifying the complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects; and
Require the MGSO to maintain RLRN, RPCRNS, LRN Property Card, and Local Road map in the standard format.

We recommended that the LCE direct:
the MA, MGSO, and Municipal Assessor to collaborate in reconciling the balance of the land account in their respective records;
the Municipal Assessor to conduct ocular inspection of all lands with TCTs and TDs; and
the Municipal Budget Officer to appropriate adequate funds to be used in securing land titles under the name of the MGB.
Management Comment:
Management is already in the process of complying with some audit recommendations in the succeeding year.

We recommended that the LCE:
Direct the MGSO to conduct physical count of all inventory items every semester and prepare the RPCI within the prescribed period as prescribed by the Manuals on NGAS for LGUs. Thereafter, require the MGSO and the MA to reconcile the RPCI and the accounting records of any variance;
Require the MGSO and the MA to maintain properly and update SCs and SLCs, respectively, and reconcile their records regularly; and
Direct the MGSO to adopt strictly the Perpetual Inventory Method wherein all deliveries, inspection, storage, and stock monitoring of common-used goods and supplies shall be done by the MGSO pursuant to Section 114 of the Manual on NGAS for LGUs. All Department Heads shall be informed of such system/method.
Management Comment:
Management agreed to put additional personnel in the MGSO to implement the proper conduct of physical inventory and reconciliation of records.

We recommended that the LCE require the MA to verify thoroughly the status of long outstanding fund transfers still maintained in the books and require the MT to remit to the SAs any unutilized amount pursuant to their existing Memorandum of Agreement. For those fund transfers without identified SAs, remit all account balances to the Bureau of Treasury.
Management Comment:
The Management through the MAO has already sent confirmation letters to various NGAs. Unfortunately, only those agencies with recently downloaded funds responded to the letter.  As a result, the beginning balances are still unaccounted.

We recommended and Management agreed to require the MT to deposit immediately all undeposited collections particularly the balance of ₱4.98 million as of year-end and see to it that all collections are deposited daily and intact as mandated by existing rules and regulations.
We also recommended and Management directed the MA and the MT to investigate the unreconciled amount in the Cash Local Treasury account for all funds and prepare the necessary adjustments to reconcile the accounting and the treasury records. Thereafter, reconciliation of both records shall be done at least quarterly.

We recommended that the LCE:
Demand from the previous MA to surrender the pertinent accounting records to the incumbent MA;
Require the MA and MT to retrieve pertinent data from cashbooks and accounting records to substantiate the receivables; and
Require the MA to send demand letters based on the data gathered.
If collectivity is remote, we recommend that management request from COA for write off of the dormant receivables supported with documents required under COA Circular No. 93-404 to present a reliable balance of Receivable accounts in the FS.
Management Comment:
Management has already tried to comply with the audit observations but encountered some difficulties such as non-cooperation of previous MA to turnover properly related documents and records.

We recommended that Management maximize strictly the utilization of the 20% DF and implement the PPAs within the targeted timeframe to achieve the desired social, economic, and environmental outcomes essential in the attainment of quality life.
Management Comment:
There were delays in the approval of funding by the Sangguniang Bayan and the project design, but all unimplemented PPAs were completed within the first semester of CY 2022.

We recommended that Management ensure that the identified priority PPAs are implemented as planned and to utilize fully the LDRRMF within the scheduled timeframes. In such manner, the in-house capability of the LGU to efficiently manage all types of emergencies and to achieve orderly transitions from response to a sustained recovery in case of calamity will be strengthened.
Management Comment:
Management agreed with the audit observations and recommendations. The Municipal DRRM Office (MDRRMO), as the focal Department and the Secretariat to the MDRRM Council continues to improve the services needed by the constituents and thereby deliver the mandate accorded by RA 10121.

We recommended that Management stop the practice of charging LDRRMF expenses that are not related to and do not support disaster risk management activities, and strictly adhere to the provisions of NDRRMC-DBM-DILG JMC Nos. 2013-1 and 2014-1.
Management Comment:
The Management has already provided a budget in the CY 2022 GF to cover the administrative expenses of the MDRRMO.

We also recommended that Management ensure that the procedures in the post-closure land use plan be carried out strictly. These include inspection, maintenance, and monitoring after the implementation of closure activities which shall maintain the integrity and compliance of the closed dumpsite.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to:
Direct the GAD Focal Person to facilitate the early submission of GPB and GAR;
Direct the GFPS and Municipal Budget Office to reconcile information of GPB; and
Send a competent staff/member of the GFPS to training on how to use monitoring tools such as the HGDG and PIMME in monitoring and evaluation of results of GAD programs, projects, and activities that would aid in assessing the effectiveness and efficiency of the LGU gender mainstreaming efforts.

observations.recommendation:

Reconcile thoroughly the balances per book and per bank to determine the valid reconciling items and prepare the necessary adjustments in the books for all the errors committed in the books and notify the bank immediately of any errors they may have committed in order to arrive at a correct Cash in Bank account;

Exercise due diligence and professional care in the conduct of reconciliation of records and in the performance of functions especially in the recording of journal entries and mathematical computation thereof.

Thereafter, a reconciliation of both records shall be made on a monthly basis.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

Require all the concerned AOs to settle their outstanding cash advances immediately by demanding the submission of liquidation reports and supporting documents. Otherwise, cause the withholding or suspension of their salaries for non-compliance therewith;

Strengthen the control measures to ensure timely submission of liquidation documents and full accounting of balances of cash advances by strictly monitoring the period of liquidation and impose sanctions on AOs who are unable to liquidate on time; and

Strictly comply with the provisions of Section 89 of PD No. 1445 and COA Circular No. 97-002 on the granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our previous year’s audit recommendations that Management:
Require the MGSO and the Municipal Accounting Office to prioritize the reconciliation process and ensure that discrepancies noted are immediately investigated and adjusted in the books accordingly; and
Require the Municipal Accounting Office to maintain complete and updated PPE Ledger Cards to support GL account balances and the MGSO to keep corresponding PCs for control purposes.
We also recommended that the Municipal Accounting Office trace back prior years’ acquisitions to have complete details of the PPE accounts that will serve as basis in the computation of depreciation, in order to present true value of the depreciable PPE in the FS.

We recommended that Management:
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to continue and strengthen coordination with the Municipal Engineer to determine the condition of the infrastructure assets constructed in CYs 2015 and below, in order to assess the carrying amounts of the said assets and effect the necessary adjustments in the books to arrive at a more reliable Infrastructure Asset accounts balance in the FS;
Require the Municipal Engineer to provide the Inventory Committee with complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects to facilitate the preparation of RPCLRN; and
Direct the Inventory Committee and MGSO to prepare and submit the RPCLRN and RLRN, respectively, in accordance with the prescribed formats as provided under COA Circular No. 2015-008.
We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the MGSO prepare and maintain separate subsidiary records/LRNLCs and LRNPCs, respectively, for local road projects to ensure proper recording and control of the assets.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Treasurer to deposit immediately all undeposited collections particularly the balance of  ₱9.97 million as of year-end and see to it that all collections are deposited daily and intact as mandated by existing rules and regulations.
We also recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to investigate the unreconciled amount in the Cash Local Treasury accounts for all funds and prepare the necessary adjustments to reconcile the accounting and the treasury records.  Thereafter, a reconciliation of both records be made at least quarterly.

We reiterated our prior years’ audit recommendations that Management exhaust all possible means to require the concerned officers and employees the liquidation or settlement of their outstanding cash advances including, but not limited  to the following:
Demand from the concerned AOs the immediate submission of duly accomplished liquidation reports or to refund the cash still in their possession; and
Institute appropriate legal actions/sanctions such as suspension of salaries of those erring AOs/employees for the continued non-compliance with the existing laws, rules and regulations on the granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances, if warranted.
Also, we reiterated that Management strengthen the operation of preventive and monitoring controls to ensure correct recording of cash advances and timely submission of liquidation documents for full accounting of balances of all cash advances.
We also recommended that Management exert extra effort to retrieve the documents relative to long outstanding cash advances as basis for the issuance of demand letter by the Municipal Accountant.
Likewise, we recommended that Management reconcile the GL and SL balances of the Advances to Officers and Employees account in order to address the negative balances.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to meticulously verify the status of long outstanding fund transfers still maintained in its books and require the Municipal Treasurer to remit to the grantor-agency any unutilized amount pursuant to their existing Memorandum of Agreement.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant to carefully check the entries made in the subsidiary ledgers to determine the cause/s of the discrepancy/ies and to immediately make the necessary correcting entries to bring both accounts in agreement and to ensure the correctness and accuracy of the reciprocal accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended that:
The MGSO initiate the conduct of physical inventory taking of all Supplies of the Municipality at year-end and to prepare and submit the corresponding RPCI to the Audit Team in order to establish the existence, accuracy and validity of reported balances in the financial statements; and
The Municipal Accountant and MGSO maintain a complete and updated Stock Cards and SLCs and regularly conduct reconciliation thereof so that any discrepancies noted could be promptly adjusted and reconciled.

We recommended that Management:
Require the Municipal Accountant to strengthen accounting controls over Cash.  The unrecorded disbursements/deposits be booked up in the General Journal by drawing a JEV. This is necessary to take up the appropriate adjusting entries for all reconciling items noted in the BRS. These shall be supported with complete and proper documents;
Require the Municipal Accountant to coordinate closely with the Municipal Treasurer’s Office to ensure that all disbursements/deposits for a given period are booked up and recorded in the books of the Municipality. In addition, bank confirmation be made regularly to validate actual existence of the accounts; and
Request from concerned depository banks, copies of the debit and credit memos to determine their validity and effect the necessary adjustments to correct the affected accounts balance.

We recommended that Management strictly maximize the utilization of the 20 per cent Development Fund and implement the PPAs within the targeted timeframe to achieve the social, economic and environmental outcomes essential in the attainment of the constitutional objective of a desired quality of life for all.

We recommended that the Municipal SWM Board:
Exert more effort to expedite the approval of the 10-year SWM Plan 2019-2028;
Prioritize the implementation of SWM program; and
Exercise strong political will to implement the environmental laws on preserving and protecting the environment as mandated under RA No. 9003.

We recommended that Management require the LSB to prepare the LSB three-year program and the recommendation of the DepEd PSDS and SDS as well as the required SIPs and DEDPs and other information from DepEd RO, which supposedly were the bases for the preparation and approval by the LSB of the SEF Budget and furnish the Audit Team for reference and review purposes.  Henceforth, the LSB must strictly follow the mandated required processes and documents in planning and budgeting for SEF under the revised guidelines on the use of SEF pursuant to the above-mentioned JC.
Likewise, we recommended that the LSB strictly comply with the requirements of the said JC in identifying the PPAs that reflect an equitable distribution of the mandated priority expenditures and refrain from using the SEF outside the scope for which it was established.
Finally, justification is requested why the non-eligible expenditures may not be disallowed in audit.

We recommended that Management:
Create IAS unit, under the administrative supervision and control of the Municipal Mayor to institute and conduct a program of internal audit for the agency, in compliance with Budget Circular No. 2004-4 to ensure that the government spends within means, invests in the right priorities, and delivers measurable results for greater transparency and accountability; and
Adopt the IAM for LGUs as a guide in establishing, maintaining and operating an IAS/U, pursuant to DBM Local Budget Circular No. 110 dated June 10, 2016.
We also recommended that the concerned Municipal officials submit a proposal to the Sangguniang Bayan for the establishment of the Internal Audit Service/Unit in the Municipality, including the organizational structure, functional charts, personnel complement, operating budget and funding source.

We recommended and Management agreed to update the Revenue Code of the Municipality pursuant to Section 191 of RA No. 7160 to increase the revenue collection of local taxes that can be used to finance more valuable projects of the Municipality.

Ensure that all valid reconciling items to be recorded and adjusted in the books are properly supported with complete documents; and
Exercise due diligence and professional care in the conduct of reconciliation of records and in the performance of functions particularly in the recording of journal entries and mathematical computation thereof. 
We likewise reiterated our prior years’ recommendation that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer fully investigate the unreconciled balances of the Cash in Bank - LCCA accounts for all funds and prepare the necessary adjustments to reconcile the accounting and the treasury records. Thereafter, a reconciliation of both records shall be made on a monthly basis.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our prior years’ recommendations that the Municipal Accountant:

Conduct a thorough review of the Accounts Payable account as of year-end, maintain SLs and periodically reconcile with the controlling account in the GL and prepare an aging with complete details as to the date incurred, creditor and particulars; and

Observe strictly the proper classification of accounts consistent with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2015-009.

We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the Municipal Budget Officer, ensure that all liabilities are taken up in the books at year-end.

Further, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant review the correctness and validity of the accounting entries and postings made in the books of accounts to achieve accuracy of the Accounts Payable account balance.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the Municipal Treasurer:

Conduct a thorough review of the Due to NGAs account as of year-end, reconcile the discrepancies noted in audit and effect the necessary adjustments in the books;

Exert effort in establishing the beginning balances of each trust fund to facilitate the preparation and maintenance of SLs and periodic reconciliation with the controlling account in the GL; and

Ensure the correctness and validity of the accounting entries and postings made in the books of accounts to achieve accuracy of the Due to NGAs account balance.

We recommended that Management ensure that the 20 per cent Development Fund is used solely for development projects and no amount is utilized for expenditure items not allowed to be charged under the DILG and DBM JMC No. 2017-1.

We recommended that the HRMO:

Ensure that the hiring of workers under contract of service require expertise that cannot be performed by the regular employees of the Municipality;
Review the contracts and include, among others, the justification for the need of hiring individuals under contract of service, the terms of engagement, timelines and the expected deliverables/outputs; and

Require the submission of CV or PDS and accomplishment report as bases for evaluation of their qualifications, expertise or specialization to undertake the special project or job and payment of honoraria, respectively.

We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that Management require the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to exert extra effort in preparing and submitting on time the subject accounts and financial reports including the supporting documents. We suggested that such activities be properly monitored so that reports are submitted promptly in accordance with the salient provisions of PD No. 1445 and COA Circular No. 2009-006.
observations.reason: The high turnover of employees greatly affected the preparation of reports.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

Provide the necessary training for SWM personnel to ensure that solid wastes are properly handled and that the risk of hazards to them and to the public are avoided or minimized; and 
 
Observe the standards and requirements for the collection, transport and handling of solid wastes as required under the IRR of RA No. 9003.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our prior years’ recommendations that Management: 

Require the BAC, the Secretariat and the TWG to strictly abide by the standards set forth by RA No. 9184, its Revised IRR and other relevant laws on government procurement;  
Require them also to exercise due diligence in the performance of their duties to ensure that all requirements of the procurement law are faithfully adhered to; and
Direct the Municipal Accountant to observe the requirements of COA Circular No. 2012-001 at all times to avoid suspension or disallowance in audit.
Also, we recommended that Management observe the requirements of COA Circular No. 2009-001 regarding the submission of copies of approved contracts and their supporting documents to the Auditor within five working days from perfection thereof.

We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that the LSB comply strictly with the requirements of Section 272 of RA No. 7160 and DECS-DBM-DILG Joint Circular Nos. 01 s. 1998, 01-A s. 2000 and 01-B s. 2001, as follows:

Identify the PPAs that reflect an equitable distribution of the mandated priority expenses and refrain from using the SEF outside the scope for which it was established to avoid disallowance in audit;
Maximize the implementation of the identified and prioritized PPAs to ensure efficient utilization of the fund so that its objectives are fully achieved; and
Strictly monitor the budget to anticipate the timely re-alignment of funds for other allowable PPAs in lieu of those that cannot be implemented.

We reiterated our prior year’s recommendations that Management: 

Expedite the completion/updating of the 10-year Municipal SWM Plan for approval by the NSWMC to serve as guide for the efficient management of solid waste generated within the Municipality’s jurisdiction; and
Strictly comply with RA No. 9003 on the establishment of solid waste facility.

We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that Management direct the Municipal Treasurer to deposit immediately all undeposited collections and see to it that all collections are deposited daily and intact as mandated by existing rules and regulations, otherwise withhold the salary of the accountable officer until such time that all collections are deposited.

We also recommended that Management require the OIC - Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to:

Investigate the unreconciled amounts in the Cash - Local Treasury accounts for all funds and effect the necessary adjustments to reconcile the accounting and the treasury records; and

Reconcile their respective records regularly in order to present more reliable financial information.

We recommended that the OIC - Municipal Accountant determine the unrecorded RPT for CYs 2014 and 2015 and thereafter, effect the necessary adjustments in the books including the amount of P4,670,807.68 for SET to reflect the actual income of the Municipality on RPT/SET.

Also, it was recommended that strict compliance with the existing government accounting rules and regulations be observed at all times to avoid any errors in the recording of collections.

We recommended that Management observe the requirements of NDRRMC-DBM-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1 at all times to ensure that the LDRRMF is used strictly for disaster risk reduction and management activities. 

Also, we recommended that the OIC - Municipal Accountant warrant that all disbursements are supported with proper documentation prior to payment to avoid disallowance or suspension in audit.

Further, it was recommended that Management limit its disbursements up to the amount of appropriation in the annual budget as approved and reviewed, otherwise overspending may be disallowed in audit.

We recommended that Management refrain from using the SEF outside the scope for which it was established and that the OIC - Municipal Accountant warrant that all disbursements are supported with proper documentation prior to payment to avoid future disallowance or suspension in audit.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the OIC - Municipal Accountant devise strategies for the prevention and correction of any inaccuracies in the financial records such as, but not limited to the following:

Review all prepared journal entries to avoid mistakes/errors in the recording of the transactions; and

Ensure that a reconciliation of RPT and SET Receivable account balances is done regularly.

We recommended that Management conduct a complete physical count of all the unserviceable properties, prepare the IIRUP and immediately dispose the said properties in accordance with COA Circular No. 92-386 on the disposal of unserviceable properties.

We recommended that Management instruct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to record the grant of cash advances in accordance with COA Circular No.  97-002. Likewise, we recommended that the Disbursing Officer take up the cash advances in her cashbook for proper accountability.

We recommended that Management:

Direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to investigate the unreconciled amount in the Cash in Vault (101) accounts for all funds and prepare the necessary adjustments to reconcile the accounting and the treasury records.  Thereafter, a reconciliation of both records be made at least quarterly; and

Remind the OIC-Municipal Accountant to ensure the correctness and completeness of the recorded transactions affecting Cash in Vault (101) account for all funds in accordance with Section 48 of GAAM Volume III.

We recommended that Management limit its disbursements up to the amount of appropriation in the annual budget as approved and reviewed, otherwise overpayments may be disallowed in audit.  We likewise recommended that the concerned officials strictly observe the provisions under Section 336 of RA No. 7160 that transfer of appropriations within the same class be made upon the passage of an ordinance authorizing the same.

Furthermore, it was recommended that the Accounting Office and the Budget Office reconcile their records to produce more reliable information needed in budget review.

We recommended that Management:

Require the OIC-Municipal Accountant effect the proposed adjusting journal entry to correct the double recording of liquidation of payroll for salaries and wages and barangay augmentation amounting to ₱2,791,680.00.

Instruct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to record expenses using the appropriate accounts as prescribed in the Manual on NGAS for LGUs; and 

Ensure that all payments or allowances, incentives, and other benefits to all officials and employees shall be based on and conform strictly with laws, rules and regulations granting or authorizing such payments as reiterated in COA Circular No. 2013-003.

We recommended that a duly certified Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF be submitted on or before the 15th day after the end of each month by the LDRRMO through the LDRRMC and LDC to the Office of the Auditor and other government offices in compliance with the provisions of Republic Act No. 10121 and COA Circular No. 2012-002.

We recommended that Management observe the existing rules and regulations on the purchase of vehicles by LGUs. Also, the BAC should ensure that the provisions of RA No. 9184 or the New Procurement Law are strictly followed and the Municipal Treasurer/Accountant should warrant the completeness of supporting documents before transactions are paid/recorded to avoid future suspension or disallowance in audit.

We recommended that the OIC-Municipal Accountant review the entries posted in the General Ledger with reference to the General Journal and the special journals to arrive at the correct balances of the affected accounts and make the necessary adjustments. 

Further, it is recommended that a careful review be made in recording/posting entries in the journals and the General Ledger to report accurately the financial transactions and information of the Municipality.

We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that the Municipality should closely monitor its cash position to meet its present obligations. Also, it must adopt measures to improve its financial viability such as intensifying the collection efforts of the Municipal Treasurer’s Office on the taxes due the public, the Real Property and Special Education Taxes Receivable in particular which are very huge in amount; and continuously adopting the austerity measures in the government under Administrative Order No. 103 dated August 31, 2004 to avoid over-incurrence of expenditures.

We recommended that Management: 

Instruct the Municipal Treasurer to exert extra efforts to collect these loans in order for the program to continue reaching out to more people who need the assistance; 

Review the collectability of these loans receivable, especially the balances due from cooperatives which are no longer operating or dissolved or whose registrations have been cancelled; and 

Consider requesting from the Commission on Audit for an authority to write-off those accounts which have been dormant/non-moving for more than 10 years and deemed uncollectible pursuant to COA Res. No. 2003-002 dated January 30, 2003.
